records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3618 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"A Muslim cannot be righteous except through three qualities: understanding the religion, managing livelihood wisely, and exercising patience in adversity."
records.arabicText: 3618 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لاَ يَصْلُحُ اَلْمَرْءُ اَلْمُسْلِمُ إِلاَّ بِثَلاَثٍ اَلتَّفَقُّهِ فِي اَلدِّينِ وَ اَلتَّقْدِيرِ فِي اَلْمَعِيشَةِ وَ اَلصَّبْرِ عَلَى اَلنَّائِبَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3618 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A Muslim cannot be righteous except through three qualities: understanding the religion, managing livelihood wisely, and exercising patience in adversity."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3619 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "Indeed, when the soul secures its provision, it becomes content."
records.arabicText: 3619 - قَالَ وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "إِنَّ اَلنَّفْسَ إِذَا أَحْرَزَتْ قُوتَهَا اِسْتَقَرَّتْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3619 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, when the soul secures its provision, it becomes content."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3620 - Mu'ammar ibn Khallad asked Abu al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) about storing food for a year.
Imam (as) replied: "I do it myself."
records.arabicText: 3620 - وَ سَأَلَ مُعَمَّرُ بْنُ خَلاَّدٍ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ حَبْسِ اَلطَّعَامِ سَنَةً فَقَالَ "أَنَا أَفْعَلُهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3620 - Mu'ammar ibn Khallad asked Abu al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) about storing food for a year.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) replied: "I do it myself."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3621 - Ibn Abi Ya'fur narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
"The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
'There is no spending more beloved to Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, than moderate spending. He (swt) dislikes extravagance, except in Hajj and Umrah.
May Allah (swt) have mercy on a believer who earns lawfully, spends moderately, or gives something in charity from his surplus.' "
records.arabicText: 3621 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي يَعْفُورٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَالَ : "مَا مِنْ نَفَقَةٍ أَحَبَّ إِلَى اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مِنْ نَفَقَةِ قَصْدٍ وَ يُبْغِضُ اَلْإِسْرَافَ إِلاَّ فِي اَلْحَجِّ وَ اَلْعُمْرَةِ فَرَحِمَ اَللَّهُ مُؤْمِناً كَسَبَ طَيِّباً وَ أَنْفَقَ مِنْ قَصْدٍ أَوْ قَدَّمَ فَضْلاً ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3621 - Ibn Abi Ya'fur narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
'There is no spending more beloved to Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, than moderate spending. He (swt) dislikes extravagance, except in Hajj and Umrah.
May Allah (swt) have mercy on a believer who earns lawfully, spends moderately, or gives something in charity from his surplus.' "

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3622 - The Scholar, Imam (as) said: "I guarantee that whoever practices moderation will never become poor."
records.arabicText: 3622 - وَ قَالَ اَلْعَالِمُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "ضَمِنْتُ لِمَنِ اِقْتَصَدَ أَنْ لاَ يَفْتَقِرَ".

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3623 - Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as) said:
"A man may spend his wealth on what is right, yet still be considered extravagant."
records.arabicText: 3623 - وَ قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِنَّ اَلرَّجُلَ لَيُنْفِقُ مَالَهُ فِي حَقٍّ وَ إِنَّهُ لَمُسْرِفٌ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3623 - Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A man may spend his wealth on what is right, yet still be considered extravagant."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3624 - Al-Asbagh ibn Nubata narrated from Commander of the Faithful (as) that he said:
"The extravagant person has three signs: he eats what does not belong to him, buys what does not belong to him, and wears what does not belong to him."
records.arabicText: 3624 - وَ رَوَى اَلْأَصْبَغُ بْنُ نُبَاتَةَ عَنْ أَمِيرِ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "لِلْمُسْرِفِ ثَلاَثُ عَلاَمَاتٍ يَأْكُلُ مَا لَيْسَ لَهُ وَ يَشْتَرِي مَا لَيْسَ لَهُ وَ يَلْبَسُ مَا لَيْسَ لَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3624 - Al-Asbagh ibn Nubata narrated from Commander of the Faithful (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The extravagant person has three signs: he eats what does not belong to him, buys what does not belong to him, and wears what does not belong to him."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3625 - Abu Hisham al-Basri narrated from Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) that he said:
"Among the causes of corruption are the discontinuation of currency (dirham and dinar) and the discarding of date pits."
records.arabicText: 3625 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو هِشَامٍ اَلْبَصْرِيُّ عَنْ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "مِنَ اَلْفَسَادِ قَطْعُ اَلدِّرْهَمِ وَ اَلدِّينَارِ وَ طَرْحُ اَلنَّوَى ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3625 - Abu Hisham al-Basri narrated from Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Among the causes of corruption are the discontinuation of currency (dirham and dinar) and the discarding of date pits."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3626 - Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the least form of extravagance. Imam (as) replied: "Wearing your garment of dignity for ordinary tasks, spilling the excess of what is in your vessel, and carelessly throwing away date pits like this and that."
records.arabicText: 3626 - وَ سَأَلَ إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ أَدْنَى اَلْإِسْرَافِ فَقَالَ "ثَوْبُ صَوْنِكَ تَبْتَذِلُهُ وَ فَضْلُ اَلْإِنَاءِ تُهَرِيقُهُ وَ قَذْفُكَ اَلنَّوَى هَكَذَا وَ هَكَذَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3626 - Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah (as) about the least form of extravagance. Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Wearing your garment of dignity for ordinary tasks, spilling the excess of what is in your vessel, and carelessly throwing away date pits like this and that."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3627 - Al-Walid ibn Sabih narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that he said:
"Three people make supplications, but their prayers are not answered"-or he said: "their prayers are returned to them":
A man who had a large amount of wealth, reaching thirty thousand or forty thousand, and spent it without careful consideration. Then he says, 'O Allah (swt), provide for me.' Allah (swt), the Exalted, says, 'Did I not provide for you?'
A man who refrains from seeking sustenance and says, 'O Allah (swt), provide for me.' Allah (swt), the Exalted, says, 'Did I not make a way for you to seek provision?'
A man who had a wife and prays, 'O Allah (swt), separate me from her.' Allah (swt), the Almighty, says, 'Did I not give you the authority to handle this matter yourself?'"
records.arabicText: 3627 - وَ رَوَى اَلْوَلِيدُ بْنُ صَبِيحٍ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "ثَلاَثَةٌ يَدْعُونَ فَلاَ يُسْتَجَابُ لَهُمْ" أَوْ قَالَ "يُرَدُّ عَلَيْهِمْ دُعَاؤُهُمْ رَجُلٌ كَانَ لَهُ مَالٌ كَثِيرٌ يَبْلُغُ ثَلاَثِينَ أَلْفاً أَوْ أَرْبَعِينَ أَلْفاً فَأَنْفَقَهُ فِي وُجُوهِهِ فَيَقُولُ اَللَّهُمَّ اُرْزُقْنِي فَيَقُولُ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى أَ لَمْ أَرْزُقْكَ وَ رَجُلٌ أَمْسَكَ عَنِ اَلطَّلَبِ فَيَقُولُ اَللَّهُمَّ اُرْزُقْنِي فَيَقُولُ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى أَ لَمْ أَجْعَلْ لَكَ اَلسَّبِيلَ إِلَى اَلطَّلَبِ وَ رَجُلٌ كَانَتْ عِنْدَهُ اِمْرَأَةٌ فَقَالَ اَللَّهُمَّ فَرِّقْ بَيْنِي وَ بَيْنَهَا فَيَقُولُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَ لَمْ أَجْعَلْ ذَلِكَ إِلَيْكَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3627 - Al-Walid ibn Sabih narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Three people make supplications, but their prayers are not answered"-or he said: "their prayers are returned to them":
A man who had a large amount of wealth, reaching thirty thousand or forty thousand, and spent it without careful consideration. Then he says, 'O Allah (swt), provide for me.' Allah (swt), the Exalted, says, 'Did I not provide for you?'
A man who refrains from seeking sustenance and says, 'O Allah (swt), provide for me.' Allah (swt), the Exalted, says, 'Did I not make a way for you to seek provision?'
A man who had a wife and prays, 'O Allah (swt), separate me from her.' Allah (swt), the Almighty, says, 'Did I not give you the authority to handle this matter yourself?'"

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3628 - Imam (as) said: "Part of a man's happiness is that he is the caretaker of his family."
records.arabicText: 3628 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مِنْ سَعَادَةِ اَلْمَرْءِ أَنْ يَكُونَ اَلْقَيِّمَ عَلَى عِيَالِهِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3628 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Part of a man's happiness is that he is the caretaker of his family."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3629 - Imam (as) said: "It is sufficient for a man to be sinful if he neglects those he is responsible for."
records.arabicText: 3629 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "كَفَى بِالْمَرْءِ إِثْماً أَنْ يُضَيِّعَ مَنْ يَعُولُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3629 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is sufficient for a man to be sinful if he neglects those he is responsible for."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3630 - The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"Cursed, cursed is the one who neglects those he is responsible for."
records.arabicText: 3630 - وَ قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "مَلْعُونٌ مَلْعُونٌ مَنْ يُضَيِّعُ مَنْ يَعُولُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3630 - The Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Cursed, cursed is the one who neglects those he is responsible for."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3631 - Imam (as) said: "The one who strives to provide for his family through lawful means is like a warrior in the path of Allah (swt)."
records.arabicText: 3631 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "اَلْكَادُّ عَلَى عِيَالِهِ مِنْ حَلاَلٍ كَالْمُجَاهِدِ فِي سَبِيلِ اَللَّهِ".

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3632 - Isma'il bin Jabir narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) that he said:
"Do not expose yourselves to obligations, but if they become binding upon you, then be patient with them."
records.arabicText: 3632 - وَ رَوَى إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "لاَ تَتَعَرَّضُوا لِلْحُقُوقِ، فَإِذَا لَزِمَتْكُمْ فَاصْبِرُوا لَهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3632 - Isma'il bin Jabir narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not expose yourselves to obligations, but if they become binding upon you, then be patient with them."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3633 - Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) said:
"Do not give to your brothers from yourself what causes you more harm than it benefits them."
records.arabicText: 3633 - وَ قَالَ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لاَ تَبْذُلْ لِإِخْوَانِكَ مِنْ نَفْسِكَ مَا ضَرَرُهُ عَلَيْكَ أَكْثَرُ مِنْ نَفْعِهِ لَهُمْ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3633 - Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Do not give to your brothers from yourself what causes you more harm than it benefits them."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3634 - Abu Abdillah (as) said:
"Beware of laziness and restlessness, for they are the keys to every evil. Indeed, whoever is lazy will not fulfill a right, and whoever is restless will not endure upon the truth."
records.arabicText: 3634 - وَ رَوَى عُمَرُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : "إِيَّاكَ وَ اَلْكَسَلَ وَ اَلضَّجَرَ فَإِنَّهُمَا مِفْتَاحُ كُلِّ سُوءٍ إِنَّهُ مَنْ كَسِلَ لَمْ يُؤَدِّ حَقّاً وَ مَنْ ضَجِرَ لَمْ يَصْبِرْ عَلَى حَقٍّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3634 - Abu Abdillah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Beware of laziness and restlessness, for they are the keys to every evil. Indeed, whoever is lazy will not fulfill a right, and whoever is restless will not endure upon the truth."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.3635 - Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) said: "Indeed, Allah (swt) the Exalted hates a servant who sleeps excessively. Indeed, Allah (swt) the Exalted hates a servant who is idle."
records.arabicText: 3635 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَعَالَى لَيُبْغِضُ اَلْعَبْدَ اَلنَّوَّامَ إِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَعَالَى لَيُبْغِضُ اَلْعَبْدَ اَلْفَارِغَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3635 - Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, Allah (swt) the Exalted hates a servant who sleeps excessively. Indeed, Allah (swt) the Exalted hates a servant who is idle."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3636 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said to Bishir al-Nabbal:
"When you are granted provision from something, remain committed to it."
records.arabicText: 3636 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ لِبَشِيرٍ اَلنَّبَّالِ : "إِذَا رُزِقْتَ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَالْزَمْهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3636 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said to Bishir al-Nabbal:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When you are granted provision from something, remain committed to it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3637 - Ishaq ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
A man complained to the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) about trade.
The Prophet said: "Look for merchandise, purchase it, and then sell it. Whatever you profit from it, remain committed to it."
records.arabicText: 3637 - وَ رَوَى إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "شَكَا رَجُلٌ إِلَى رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلْحُرْفَةَ فَقَالَ "اُنْظُرْ بُيُوعاً فَاشْتَرِهَا ثُمَّ بِعْهَا فَمَا رَبِحْتَ فِيهِ فَالْزَمْهُ" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3637 - Ishaq ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
A man complained to the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) about trade.
The Prophet said: "Look for merchandise, purchase it, and then sell it. Whatever you profit from it, remain committed to it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3638 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Handle your major affairs personally, and delegate the smaller ones to others."
It was asked: "What kind of tasks are you referring to?"
Imam (as) replied: "Matters like buying and selling properties and similar transactions."
records.arabicText: 3638 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "بَاشِرْ كِبَارَ أُمُورِكَ بِنَفْسِكَ وَ كِلْ مَا صَغُرَ مِنْهَا إِلَى غَيْرِكَ " فَقِيلَ ضَرْبُ أَيِّ شَيْءٍ فَقَالَ "ضَرْبُ أَشْرِيَةِ اَلْعَقَارِ وَ مَا أَشْبَهَهَا".

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3639 - It is narrated from Al-Arqat that Abu Abdillah (as) said:
"Do not spend your time wandering through markets, nor personally handle the purchase of minor goods. It does not befit a Muslim who possesses faith and honor to involve himself directly in purchasing small items-except in three matters.
It is proper for a man of faith and dignity to personally handle these three matters: buying real estate, camels, and slaves."
records.arabicText:
3639 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْأَرْقَطِ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ :
"لاَ تَكُونَنَّ دَوَّاراً فِي اَلْأَسْوَاقِ وَ لاَ تَلِي شِرَاءَ دَقَائِقِ اَلْأَشْيَاءِ بِنَفْسِكَ فَإِنَّهُ لاَ يَنْبَغِي لِلْمَرْءِ اَلْمُسْلِمِ ذِي اَلدِّينِ وَ اَلْحَسَبِ أَنْ يَلِيَ شِرَاءَ دَقَائِقِ اَلْأَشْيَاءِ بِنَفْسِهِ مَا خَلاَ ثَلاَثَةَ أَشْيَاءَ فَإِنَّهُ يَنْبَغِي لِذِي اَلدِّينِ وَ اَلْحَسَبِ أَنْ يَلِيَهَا بِنَفْسِهِ اَلْعَقَارَ وَ اَلْإِبِلَ وَ اَلرَّقِيقَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3639 - It is narrated from Al-Arqat that Abu Abdillah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Do not spend your time wandering through markets, nor personally handle the purchase of minor goods. It does not befit a Muslim who possesses faith and honor to involve himself directly in purchasing small items-except in three matters.
It is proper for a man of faith and dignity to personally handle these three matters: buying real estate, camels, and slaves."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3640 - Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) who said:
"Commander of the Faithful (as) used to gather firewood, draw water, and sweep, while Sayiida Fatimah {s.a} used to grind, knead, and bake."
records.arabicText:
3640 - وَ رَوَى هِشَامُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ :
"كَانَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَحْتَطِبُ وَ يَسْتَقِي وَ يَكْنُسُ وَ كَانَتْ فَاطِمَةُ عَلَيْهَا اَلسَّلاَمُ تَطْحَنُ وَ تَعْجِنُ وَ تَخْبِزُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3640 - Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Abu Abdillah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Commander of the Faithful (as) used to gather firewood, draw water, and sweep, while Sayiida Fatimah {s.a} used to grind, knead, and bake."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3641 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"The buyer of real estate is blessed, while the seller of real estate is deprived."
records.arabicText: 3641 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مُشْتَرِي اَلْعَقَارِ مَرْزُوقٌ وَ بَائِعُ اَلْعَقَارِ مَمْحُوقٌ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3641 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The buyer of real estate is blessed, while the seller of real estate is deprived."

records.chapter: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.3642 - Zurara narrated from Abu Abdillah (as): "A man does not leave behind anything more burdensome upon him than silent wealth (hoarded wealth)."
I asked him, "What should he do with it?"
Imam (as) replied: "Invest it in walls, orchards, and houses."
records.arabicText: 3642 - وَ رَوَى زُرَارَةُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "مَا يُخَلِّفُ اَلرَّجُلُ بَعْدَهُ شَيْئاً أَشَدَّ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ اَلْمَالِ اَلصَّامِتِ " قَالَ قُلْتُ لَهُ كَيْفَ يَصْنَعُ قَالَ "يَضَعُهُ فِي اَلْحَائِطِ وَ اَلْبُسْتَانِ وَ اَلدَّارِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.3642 - Zurara narrated from Abu Abdillah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"A man does not leave behind anything more burdensome upon him than silent wealth (hoarded wealth)."
I asked him, "What should he do with it?"
Imam (as) replied: "Invest it in walls, orchards, and houses."
